query: North Korea nuclear test 2026 - any signs of imminent 7th nuclear test, activity at Punggye-ri test site, recent statements or preparations mid-2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary of my findings on North Korea's potential 7th nuclear test as of mid-2026:

**Bottom line: No signs of an imminent 7th nuclear test as of early July 2026.** The Punggye-ri site is fully maintained and technically ready, but shows no test-specific preparations.

**Key findings:**
- **Site status:** Tunnel No. 3 (restored ~May 2022) is the assessed test location and has been ready since 2020 per US/ROK assessments. The latest detailed satellite analysis (Daily NK, Nov 2025) found no instruments, cables, power lines, vehicles, or personnel — only caretaker-level activity.
- **Official assessments:** IAEA's Grossi (Nov 2025) said the site "remains prepared to support a nuclear test" and reported a "rapid increase" in nuclear activity (April 2026). South Korea's DIA (Nov 2025) said a test is possible "within a short period" if Kim orders it.
- **Mid-2026 posture:** North Korea's focus has been on **fissile-material production** (new enrichment plant inaugurated June 3-4, 2026, with "exponential" expansion pledges) and **missile/artillery modernization** (June 25-26 tests) — not explosive testing.
- **Analytical nuance:** A June 2026 38 North study argues a 7th test "may not be technically necessary" given likely-achieved miniaturization. The decision rests solely with Kim, and analysts warn of **few advance indicators**, since remaining prep could be done covertly.
- **No dedicated nuclear-test prediction market** was found; a Polymarket July 2026 *missile*-test market sat near 15%.

Note: The most recent site-specific imagery analysis dates to November 2025 — no mid-2026 imagery report showing fresh test preparations was located. All findings, sources, and data points have been delivered via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.title: Punggye-ri's Tunnel No. 3 ready for use, but no signs of imminent test
sources.snippet: Nov 3, 2025 imagery: Tunnel No. 3 open but no vehicles, people, instruments, cables or power lines; no signs of an imminent test. DIA (Nov 5) said a test is physically possible if Kim orders it.

sources.title: Update on Punggye-ri Amid Increased Tension - Beyond Parallel (CSIS)
sources.snippet: Oct 4, 2024 imagery shows facility well maintained; nothing suggests imminent preparations; decision rests with Kim; timing between decision and test may be short.

sources.snippet: IAEA DG Grossi (Nov 19, 2025) noted Punggye-ri 'remains prepared to support a nuclear test.'

sources.snippet: June 3-4, 2026: Kim inaugurates new uranium enrichment facility, vows exponential arsenal expansion; Grossi (April 2026) confirmed rapid increase in nuclear activities.

sources.title: North Korea unveils a new plant to produce fuel for nuclear weapons - NPR/AP
sources.snippet: AP: North Korea hasn't carried out a 7th test since Sept 2017; June 2026 focus on fissile material production; some analysts thought 2023 tactical warhead unveiling might prelude a test.

sources.title: Revisiting North Korea's Nuclear Tests - 38 North (June 2026 analysis)
sources.snippet: Argues advanced designs likely already validated; a 7th test for the Hwasan-31 tactical warhead 'may not be technically necessary'; if tested, could validate next-gen/MIRV warheads.

sources.title: North Korea's Kim says country will exercise its position as nuclear state - Japan Times
sources.snippet: June 23, 2026 WPK Central Committee meeting: Kim says exercising nuclear-state status is the only way to cope with global security threats.

sources.title: South Korean intelligence warns North Korea can conduct seventh nuclear test
sources.snippet: Nov 5, 2025: ROK DIA told lawmakers North Korea can carry out a 7th test at Punggye-ri on short notice, pending Kim's decision.

sources.title: Press Statement of Kim Yo Jong (June 6, 2026) - KCNA
sources.snippet: June 2026: North Korea rejects 'denuclearization,' calls nuclear-weapons-state status a 'line of no retreat,' pledges to keep bolstering deterrent.

sources.title: Kim Jong Un Supervises Tests of Major Weapons (June 25, 2026) - KCNA
sources.snippet: June 25, 2026: Kim supervised tests of 240mm MLRS, tactical ballistic missile warheads, and 155mm howitzer shells — conventional focus.

sources.title: Recent Activity Observed at Punggye-ri Nuclear Test Facility - Beyond Parallel (CSIS)
sources.snippet: Jan 2024: low-level maintenance at Tunnel No. 3; US/ROK assess all 7th-test preparations completed; activity does not suggest imminent test.

sources.title: Number of North Korea Missile Tests in July 2026 - Polymarket
sources.snippet: Market on July 2026 missile (not nuclear) tests; ~15% as of July 3, 2026.

key_facts: No signs of an imminent 7th nuclear test observed at Punggye-ri as of the latest detailed imagery analysis (Daily NK, Nov 21, 2025, based on Nov 3, 2025 satellite imagery): no instruments, cables, power lines, vehicles, or personnel at Tunnel No. 3.

Tunnel No. 3 (South Portal) was restored around May 2022 and has remained open/usable; US and South Korea have assessed since 2020 that all preparations for a test there are complete — only a political decision by Kim Jong Un remains.

IAEA DG Rafael Grossi told the IAEA Board (Nov 19, 2025) that Punggye-ri 'remains prepared to support a nuclear test'; in April 2026 he reported a 'rapid increase' in North Korean nuclear-facility activity.
South Korea's Defense Intelligence Agency (Nov 5, 2025 closed-door audit) assessed North Korea could conduct a nuclear test 'within a short period of time' at Tunnel No. 3 if Kim orders it.

On June 3-4, 2026, Kim Jong Un inaugurated a new uranium-enrichment/nuclear-materials factory, vowing to expand nuclear forces 'at an exponential rate' and claiming weapons-grade material capacity has 'more than doubled' vs five years ago — focus on production, not testing.
On June 23, 2026, Kim reaffirmed exercising North Korea's 'position as a nuclear state'; on June 25-26, 2026 he supervised conventional artillery and missile tests, not nuclear activity.

A June 2026 38 North analysis argues a 7th test 'may not be technically necessary' because North Korea likely already achieved sufficient warhead miniaturization; the untested Hwasan-31 tactical warhead (unveiled March 2023) is the most anticipated test candidate.

North Korea's last (6th) nuclear test was September 2017; prior tests: Oct 2006, May 2009, Feb 2013, Jan 2016, Sep 2016. The decision on a 7th test rests solely with Kim Jong Un, with analysts warning of few advance indicators.
North Korea continues to reject denuclearization talks, demanding recognition as a nuclear-weapons state as a precondition (Kim Yo Jong statements June 2026; Arms Control Association April 2026).

data_points.metric: Punggye-ri tunnels; number currently usable (Tunnel No. 3)
Kim Jong Un claimed increase in weapons-grade nuclear material production capacity vs 5 years ago
Polymarket probability of North Korea missile-test threshold in July 2026 (NOT nuclear)
